narratives.todays_take: DHR trades at Structural Review (40.0), a 50-point collapse driven by institutional exodus and negative price momentum. Institutional Confidence scores 4.5—the binding pillar—with 13F holders distributing (-0.96 weighted signal) and insiders executing heavy selling (conviction -0.82, net -$512k). Thesis Integrity anchors at 75.8 (bullish sentiment 26/30 articles), but cannot offset the institutional abandonment and -28.8% 90-day momentum.
narratives.why_changed: The 8-point one-day drift reflects a sharp institutional reset: net 8 sellers vs. 1 buyer among the 9-manager cohort; BlackRock, Janus Henderson, and Point72 are net distributing with a -3.08% share decline in Q1 2026. Director Alan G. Spoon executed open-market sales ($237k+) on 2026-05-01, signaling insider confidence deterioration. Adoption Momentum (36.6) remains structurally weak at 2.9% YoY revenue growth and -1.7% QoQ acceleration.
narratives.why_not_to_sell: Thesis Integrity (75.8) maintains conviction via heavy bullish media coverage (86.7% positive; 30-article sample, mean sentiment +0.60) and active SEC 8K disclosure. Financial Evolution (54.6) shows a bright OCF margin of 26.6% (91.6 subscore), offsetting weak gross margin and revenue stasis. The binding pillar—Institutional Confidence at 4.5—is data-complete (holdings available, insider available, momentum calculated), so the institutional signal is not a measurement gap but a real structural shift.
narratives.what_would_break: A drop below 40 (current Structural Review threshold) requires only a further 4.0-point composite decline, which one more quarter of insider selling or a 13F quarterly distribution shift could trigger. The Monitor band threshold is 60; reaching that would require Institutional Confidence to recover to ~30 (a full reversal of current distributing regime) or Adoption Momentum to accelerate sharply (currently -1.7% QoQ suggests further deceleration risk). Macro overlay is neutral-to-supportive (yield curve +0.54%, HY OAS 2.83%, no stress), so the thesis break is endogenous—execution and capital allocation, not macro.

narratives.todays_take: DUK lands in Monitor (65.7) anchored by a collapsing Demand Environment (34.4) that overwhelms otherwise healthy Adoption Momentum (90.3) and Financial Evolution (84.1). The macro overlay—weak consumer sentiment (53.3), elevated unemployment (8.2 U-6), and negative real yields (-3.9%)—is the binding constraint; the utility is still growing revenue 35% YoY and generating exceptional OCF margins (33.8%), but the operating environment has turned structurally hostile.
narratives.why_changed: DUK drifted +1.0 today but is down 6.1 over thirty days, signaling recent consolidation within a weakening macro regime. The demand environment component cratered as CPI momentum turned negative, real yields compressed, and consumer sentiment deteriorated; this single pillar drop is responsible for the band downshift from Constructive into Monitor territory. Adoption and financial metrics remain intact, but they are now insufficient to offset macro headwinds.
narratives.why_not_to_sell: The binding pillar (Demand Environment at 34.4) reflects macro stress, not franchise decay; DUK's revenue is accelerating 35.6% YoY and OCF subscore is maxed at 100, indicating the business is still compounding cash cleanly even as sector conditions weaken. However, the data_quality gaps are material: Google Trends slope is null (missing 15+ data points), institutional holdings are only partial coverage (65% of tracked AUM, 9-manager cohort), and sentiment is pulled from just 29 articles with mean relevance at 1.0—these gaps mean the true demand picture may be worse or better than the 34.4 suggests.
narratives.what_would_break: If Demand Environment drops another 5 points to 29, composite slips into Weakening (50–59) band; if it collapses to sub-30, structural review territory (0–49) opens. The thesis fractures if revenue growth decelerates below 20% YoY or if OCF margin compresses below 25%—both would signal the franchise has lost its moat in a hostile operating environment. CEO and director open-market selling (conviction score −0.92, $2.9M net outflow) is the yellow flag; if cluster buying does not appear in the next 60 days, institutional confidence (63.8) will also erode into the 50s, removing a third pillar from the support structure.
